title: Full Home Joinery Fitout — Darlinghurst
summary: Compact-home joinery transformation in Darlinghurst with a custom kitchen, hidden servery, Murphy bed, concealed laundry and built-in storage.
heroAlt: Darlinghurst custom kitchen with timber-look joinery and stone splashback
overview: This Darlinghurst project shows how much can be resolved in a compact inner-city footprint when the joinery is planned properly. The rooms needed to work hard without feeling overcrowded, so the cabinetry had to carry storage, concealment and visual calm at the same time.
materials: Boston Oak-style timber-look joinery used through key parts of the fitout
Stone splashback and benchtop details in the kitchen and servery zone
Blum pocket doors concealing the hidden laundry
Concealed wall and cladding details to keep storage feeling integrated
Custom wardrobe and vanity joinery built to suit a compact footprint

afterImages.alt: Darlinghurst kitchen island with stone feature splashback
afterImages.caption: The kitchen used warm timber-look joinery and a striking stone backdrop to make a compact footprint feel more substantial.

afterImages.alt: Darlinghurst kitchen and living area with timber-look joinery
afterImages.caption: The kitchen and living zone together show how the timber-look joinery ties the open-plan space into a single resolved fitout.

afterImages.alt: Darlinghurst kitchen island with pendant lighting
afterImages.caption: The kitchen island with pendant lighting anchors the living side of the open plan and gives the room a clear focal point.

afterImages.alt: Darlinghurst hidden servery and pantry joinery
afterImages.caption: A concealed servery and pantry zone kept the everyday mess off display while still being easy to access.

afterImages.alt: Darlinghurst Murphy bed integrated into custom joinery wall
afterImages.caption: The Murphy bed let a single room perform more than one role, with cabinetry designed to keep the room calm whether the bed was in use or packed away.

afterImages.alt: Darlinghurst Murphy bed and wardrobe wall with bed folded away
afterImages.caption: With the bed folded away, the full wardrobe wall reads as a clean, resolved storage elevation that keeps the room feeling open.

afterImages.alt: Darlinghurst concealed laundry with stacked appliances
afterImages.caption: The hidden laundry used stacked appliances and Blum pocket doors so the joinery could open wide for use and close neatly after.

afterImages.alt: Darlinghurst hallway wall cladding and concealed storage
afterImages.caption: Hallway cladding gave a simple circulation space a much more finished architectural feel.

afterImages.alt: Darlinghurst vanity with warm timber joinery and stone top
afterImages.caption: The vanity carried the same material language into the bathroom so the fitout felt joined up rather than room-by-room.

afterImages.alt: Darlinghurst wardrobe joinery in timber-look finish
afterImages.caption: The wardrobe joinery used a simple, tailored front elevation to keep the room feeling uncluttered.

afterImages.alt: Darlinghurst wardrobe wall closed elevation showing full timber-look joinery
afterImages.caption: The closed wardrobe wall shows how the full-height timber-look joinery reads as a single resolved surface across the room.

beforeImages.caption: The kitchen started mid-build, showing the constraints of the apartment footprint before the joinery and finishes came together.

beforeImages.alt: Darlinghurst apartment before final kitchen joinery
beforeImages.caption: Early build-stage photos show how open and unresolved the living zone felt before the joinery started defining the space.

beforeImages.alt: Darlinghurst room before Murphy bed joinery installation
beforeImages.caption: The future Murphy bed room began as a basic open room with very little storage or flexibility.

beforeImages.alt: Darlinghurst laundry nook before concealed joinery
beforeImages.caption: The laundry zone was exposed and awkward before being wrapped in joinery and hidden behind pocketing doors.

beforeImages.alt: Darlinghurst hallway before cladding and concealed storage
beforeImages.caption: The hallway cladding was as much about resolution and finish as it was about concealment.

spaces.title: Kitchen & hidden servery
spaces.text: The kitchen anchors the project, but the hidden servery and pantry make it work. Together they provide a stronger balance of showpiece joinery and practical back-of-house storage.
spaces.alt: Darlinghurst kitchen and hidden servery zone

spaces.title: Murphy bed
spaces.text: The Murphy bed turns a compact room into a flexible space that can work for guests, study or everyday living without surrendering storage or floor area.

spaces.title: Hidden laundry
spaces.text: Stacked appliances, concealed shelving and pocketing doors let the laundry disappear cleanly when not in use — a major win in a compact apartment.
spaces.alt: Darlinghurst concealed laundry with stacked washer and dryer

spaces.title: Hallway cladding
spaces.text: The hallway cladding was designed to make transitional space feel deliberate. It adds warmth, visual calm and a much more finished entry sequence.
spaces.alt: Darlinghurst hallway cladding and concealed storage wall

spaces.title: Vanity & wardrobe
spaces.text: Supporting joinery in the bathroom and bedroom kept the apartment fitout feeling complete, not limited to the kitchen alone.
spaces.alt: Darlinghurst vanity with mirror and storage

outcome: The finished fitout feels surprisingly generous because the joinery does more of the organisational work, letting the apartment read cleaner and more refined.
ctaText: Need clever custom joinery ideas for a compact home, apartment or renovation? We design tailored storage solutions that improve function without adding visual noise.
